Quote: I'm on a seafood diet. I see food, and I eat it.
My bed and I are in a committed relationship.
Exercise? I thought you said extra fries.
I whisper to my Wi-Fi when it slows down.
Life is short. Smile while you still have teeth.
I followed my heart... it led me to the fridge.
Coffee understands me better than most people.
I'm not lazy; I'm on energy-saving mode.
My wallet and I are currently avoiding each other.
If Monday had a face, I'd hit snooze.
Sleep is my favorite hobby.
I'm great at giving advice I never follow.
I came. I saw. I forgot what I was doing.
Adulting should come with a pause button.
I'm not late. I'm on my own timeline.
My brain has too many tabs open.
I don't trip. I perform random gravity checks.
Chocolate never asks silly questions.
My favorite exercise is laughing at my own jokes.
I'm silently correcting everyone's grammar.
Dear alarm clock, we need to break up.
My phone battery lasts longer than my motivation.
I work hard so my pet can have a better life.
Reality called, so I put my phone on silent.
I have a degree in overthinking.
My cooking is adventurous for the fire alarm.
I smile because I have no idea what's happening.
Calories don't count on weekends. That's my story.
I need a six-month vacation twice a year.
Life gave me lemons, so I asked for salt and snacks.
My memory is excellent... until I need it.
I'm multitasking: worrying and procrastinating.
I'm not arguing. I'm explaining why I'm right.
Pizza understands my feelings.
My to-do list is very optimistic.
Common sense is becoming uncommon.
I'm allergic to mornings.
My mirror keeps giving me the same face.
Sometimes I amaze myself. Other times I lose my keys.
I'm fluent in sarcasm and snack ordering.
My bank account is practicing social distancing.
If stress burned calories, I'd be a superhero.
I survived another meeting that could've been an email.
I'm not weird. I'm limited edition.
I run on coffee, hope, and Wi-Fi.
My plant is judging my life choices.
I keep my dreams alive by hitting snooze.
Being an adult is mostly deciding what's for dinner.
My sense of humor is my best survival skill.
Smile. It confuses people in the best way.
